title: Multi-horizon hazard model with remaining-stint auxiliary target
summary: This is naturally a discrete-time event problem.
rationale: This is naturally a discrete-time event problem. Survival literature treats each step as a hazard probability, and recent F1 pit-stop work improved next-lap classification by jointly predicting pit/no-pit and remaining laps to pit. That should capture the smooth approach into a pit window better than a single binary label.
implementation_hint: Train a primary next-lap classifier plus an auxiliary target such as `laps_to_next_pit` or hazards for 1/2/3/5-lap horizons. Keep folds grouped by race and build auxiliary labels from train only. Test both a tabular booster on engineered hazard targets and a compact NN head if available.
expected_effect: Better ranking on boundary laps where the current model sees similar raw states but different proximity to the next stop.
risk: More label engineering can overfit if the CV is weak or if the auxiliary task dominates the main AUROC objective; keep OOF generation strictly fold-safe.
